QF.id: VI Functions of DP Account Opening_23

question.text: In case of joint demat accounts, joint holders may authorize:

answer.text: Any one of the holders or survivor(s) to operate the account for transfers, pledges, and other transactions
Only the first-named holder to operate the account
All holders must jointly sign every transaction
Only a nominee to operate the account

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Joint holders may authorize any one of the holders or survivor(s) to operate the account for transfers, pledges, hypothecation, and inter-depository transfers. **The Trap:** 'All holders must jointly sign' would be the default without the 'either or survivor' option. The authorization provides operational convenience. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series V-A covers joint holding options for mutual fund investments. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This is similar to the 'Either or Survivor' mode in bank accounts. It provides convenience while maintaining joint ownership.
